method: dedup-verify 16 组确认重复 -> 每组两两 LLM 合并描述(并集去重不丢信息) + triggers 并集 -> 16 合并 skill
note: 合并非剔除：22 冗余 skill 合并入 16 canonical（无损，并集描述）。只产出合并描述，不部署 ~/.claude（破坏性交人）

originalDescriptions.desc: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact wi

originalDescriptions.name: build-mcp-server
originalDescriptions.desc: This skill should be used when the user asks to "build an MCP server", "create an MCP", "make an MCP

merged.mergedDescription: Guide for building high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and integrations. Use when the user asks to "build an MCP server", "create an MCP", "make an MCP integration/app", "wrap/expose an API for Claude", or integrate external APIs/services in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK). As the entry point for MCP development, it interrogates the user about their use case, determines the right deployment model (remote HTTP, MCPB, local stdio), picks a tool-design pattern, and hands off to specialized skills.

merged.mergedDescription: Use when users want to create, write, or build a new skill, add a skill to a Claude Code plugin, modify or optimize an existing skill, improve a skill's description for better triggering accuracy, organize skill content, or run evals, benchmark performance with variance analysis, and apply development best practices. It provides guidance on proper skill structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources from scratch.

merged.mergedDescription: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build, modify, or style/beautify user-facing web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, managing state, or implementing any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that achieves a production-quality look and feel while avoiding generic AI aesthetics.

Covers project board automation, sprint planning, issue triage, PR management, CI/CD operations, release management, security monitoring, contributors, and stale items. Use when the user wants to manage GitHub issues, PRs, CI status, releases, or any GitHub operational task beyond simple git commands.

originalDescriptions.desc: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology,

originalDescriptions.name: grill-me
originalDescriptions.desc: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolvin

merged.mergedDescription: Conducts relentless, one-question-at-a-time interviews to extract what the user actually wants (vs. what they think they should want) and resolve each branch of the decision tree, until reaching shared understanding and ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Runs grilling sessions that stress-test plans and designs against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user wants to stress-test a plan/design against their project's language and documented decisions, when they explicitly invoke ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

originalDescriptions.desc:
Fast headless browser for QA testing and site dogfooding. Navigate pages, interact with
elements, ve

originalDescriptions.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
originalDescriptions.desc: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in

merged.mergedDescription: Fast headless and real browser testing toolkit (Playwright/gstack and Chrome DevTools MCP). Use when building, debugging, or testing anything that runs in a browser (e.g., QA testing, site dogfooding, verifying deployments, or opening local web apps). Navigate pages, interact with elements, inspect the DOM, verify state and frontend functionality, diff before/after, and capture browser logs. Test responsive layouts, forms, uploads, and dialogs. Analyze network requests, profile performance, and verify visual output with real runtime data. Supports capturing annotated screenshots as bug evidence for filing bugs. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured for DevTools features.

merged.mergedDescription: Use this skill when writing new features, implementing logic, fixing bugs, refactoring code, changing behavior, or modifying existing functionality. It enforces test-driven development (including bug reports) with 80%+ coverage across unit, integration, and E2E tests. Explicitly trigger this when the user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, asks for test-first development, or needs to prove code works through tests.
